title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models represent a paradigm of inference-time compute with applications to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The talk demonstrates how RLM principles can be mapped onto coding agent architectures, including through tools like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, to solve complex tasks recursively with declarable outcomes.
writeup: This talk from Raymond Weitekamp introduces Recursive Language Models (RLMs) as an inference-time compute paradigm and maps their principles onto coding agent architectures. It argues that recursion can push coding agents toward higher performance and reliability, breaking complex tasks into subtasks with declarable outcomes. Concrete references include Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse as tools that apply RLM ideas to solve tasks recursively.
